ambiguities.aspect: Classification of loan as current vs non-current liability
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: A 3-year term loan is a long-term liability; the portion due within 12 months (if any principal payments) would be current, but no principal payment schedule is stated so the full amount is recorded as long-term.
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Under IAS 1, liabilities are classified as non-current when the entity has an unconditional right to defer settlement for at least 12 months; a 3-year term loan qualifies as non-current.

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: At the time of borrowing, no interest has yet accrued; the entry records only the cash received and the loan liability.
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS requires interest expense recognition over time using the effective interest method; at inception, no interest expense is recorded.

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Loan received at face value with stated market interest rate — no discounting required as face value equals present value.
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Under IFRS 9, financial liabilities at amortized cost are initially measured at fair value; a loan at market rate has fair value equal to face value.

flags.aspect: Standard bank loan at market rate

best_attempt.reason: Cash received increases assets; loan payable is a long-term liability at face value since the loan is at market rate with no discount.
